meta.description: Emergency legal action cards — what to do in critical situations. No AI needed.

cards.title: If you are arrested
steps: Stay CALM. Do not resist physically — it is a separate offence.
ASK for the arrest memo with the officer's signature + witness signature (DK Basu 1997 mandatory).
ASK the officer to inform your family/friend. This is your right under Article 22.
DEMAND to call a lawyer (or NALSA 15100 for free legal aid).
INSIST on medical examination at time of arrest (also your right).
DO NOT sign any blank paper or confession without your lawyer present.
You MUST be produced before a Magistrate within 24 hours (excluding journey time).
Note: arresting officer's name, ID number, time + place of arrest, witnesses.

cards.title: Road accident — you or a victim
steps: CALL 112 (Emergency) and 108 (Ambulance) immediately.
Try to MOVE victim to side ONLY if there is fire/traffic risk. Else don't move spine-injured.
Take PHOTOS of vehicles, license plates, road position, victim before moving anything.
Note witness names + phone numbers.
DO NOT admit fault on the spot — let police investigate.
Stay at the scene until police arrive (leaving is hit-and-run u/s 134 MV Act).
Hit-and-run victim's family gets ₹2 lakh; injured ₹50,000 (Compensation Scheme 2022).
File MACT claim within 6 months for full insurance compensation (no upper limit since 2019).
key_laws: Motor Vehicles Act Sec 134 (duty to stop)

cards.title: Domestic violence (DV)
steps: If immediate danger: call 112 or Women Helpline 7827170170.
Go to nearest ONE STOP CENTRE (Sakhi) — they give shelter, legal aid, counselling FREE.
Document evidence: photos of injuries, screenshots of abusive messages, voice recordings.
File DV Act application u/s 12 before Magistrate — civil reliefs (protection order, residence, monetary).
Parallel FIR under IPC 498A / BNS 85 (cruelty) + Sec 506 (intimidation).
Approach Protection Officer for Domestic Incident Report (DIR).
You have RIGHT TO RESIDE in shared household — cannot be evicted.
Get medical examination + MLC (medico-legal certificate) from govt hospital — FREE.

cards.title: Lost money to online fraud / UPI scam
steps: ⚠️ CALL 1930 (Cybercrime Helpline) WITHIN 1 HOUR — RBI 'zero liability' framework allows recovery if reported quickly.
Also file complaint at https://cybercrime.gov.in
Call your bank IMMEDIATELY — ask to freeze beneficiary account.
Screenshot ALL evidence: SMS, transaction ID, beneficiary UPI, phone numbers, emails.
File FIR at nearest police station (cyber cell preferred) under IT Act 66C + 66D + IPC 420.
Block your card/UPI temporarily via bank app.
Change ALL passwords + enable 2FA on bank/UPI apps.
Don't share OTP/CVV with anyone, not even 'bank officials' — banks never ask.

cards.title: Child in danger (abuse / labour / missing)
steps: Call CHILDLINE 1098 — operates 24/7 across India.
If sexual abuse: FIR mandatory under POCSO Act. Refusal by police is a separate offence.
POCSO complaint: child statement recorded by woman officer in child-friendly setting.
Medical examination of child within 24 hours of reporting (free).
POCSO Special Court must complete trial in 1 year.
Failing to report POCSO offence is itself an offence u/s 21 POCSO Act.

cards.title: Police refusing to file FIR
steps: Submit WRITTEN complaint at SHO desk — insist on acknowledgement with diary number.
If refused: send written complaint to SP (Superintendent of Police) u/s 154(3) CrPC / 173(3) BNSS.
Keep copy of SP letter with postal/speed-post receipt.
If still no action: approach Judicial Magistrate u/s 156(3) CrPC / 175(3) BNSS — Magistrate can ORDER police to register FIR.
Or file private complaint before Magistrate u/s 200 CrPC / 223 BNSS.
Refusal to register FIR for cognizable offence is itself misconduct (Lalita Kumari 2013) + penal under Sec 166A IPC.

cards.title: Eve-teasing / sexual harassment
steps: If immediate danger: SHOUT loudly to attract attention — Section 357C CrPC mandates bystanders to assist.
Dial 112 or Women Helpline 1091.
Photograph/record the offender if safe to do so.
Note vehicle number, time, location, witnesses.
File FIR — sections vary: IPC 354A (harassment) / 354 (modesty) / 509 (insult) / 354D (stalking).
Workplace harassment: complaint to Internal Committee (IC) within 3 months — POSH Act 2013.
Women police stations exist in many cities — go directly there.
Free legal aid via NALSA 15100.

cards.title: Lost Aadhaar / PAN / Voter ID / Driving Licence
steps: AADHAAR: Reprint at https://uidai.gov.in — ₹50. No FIR needed.
PAN: Reapply at https://tin-nsdl.com — ₹107. Use 'reprint PAN' option.
VOTER ID: Apply at https://nvsp.in for duplicate EPIC. ₹25.
DRIVING LICENCE: First file LOST/STOLEN report at police station, get acknowledgement. Then apply duplicate at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in — ₹200.
PASSPORT: FIR mandatory. Then reapply at passportindia.gov.in. ₹1,500-3,500.
BANK PASSBOOK / CHEQUE BOOK: Inform bank IMMEDIATELY to freeze cheques + issue duplicate. Some banks need FIR for cheques.
Keep diary entry from police station for ALL lost documents — useful if misused.

cards.title: Defective product / bad service / fake delivery
steps: Contact seller customer care — note ticket ID, recording, screenshots.
Send written complaint via registered post / email.
File complaint on https://edaakhil.nic.in (free, fully online).
Or file at District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission within 2 YEARS of cause.
District handles up to ₹50L · State up to ₹2Cr · National >₹2Cr.
You can claim: refund / replacement / compensation / punitive damages / litigation costs.
Decision usually within 3 months. Appeal allowed.

cards.title: Death in family — what to do legally
steps: Get DEATH CERTIFICATE from hospital / municipal corporation within 21 days (free if same day, fee after).
Cancel Aadhaar / PAN / voter ID — visit respective offices with death certificate.
Inform bank — close / nominate accounts. If joint account, survivor continues.
Apply for LEGAL HEIR CERTIFICATE at Tahsildar (movables, govt dues) — ₹2-3 weeks.
Apply for SUCCESSION CERTIFICATE at District Judge (securities, debts) — 1-3 months, ₹3-5K fee.
Mutation of immovable property at Revenue Office (1-6 months).
If will exists: probate may be needed (Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata jurisdictions).
Hindu: spouse + children + mother get equal share. Daughters have EQUAL coparcenary right since 2005 (Vineeta Sharma 2020).
key_laws: Registration of Births and Deaths Act 1969

cards.title: Landlord threatening to evict / cutting water/electricity
steps: Cutting essential services (water, electricity, gas) by landlord = ILLEGAL — even if rent unpaid.
File complaint at local police station — IPC 506 (intimidation) + relevant Rent Control Act provisions.
Approach Rent Controller / Civil Court for stay on eviction.
Landlord MUST give written notice (typically 1-3 months) per agreement / Rent Act.
Eviction grounds: rent default, sub-letting, personal need, change of user, etc. — only by court order.
Do not pay rent in cash without receipt — always bank transfer for paper trail.
key_laws: State Rent Control Acts (TN 2017, MH 1999, Delhi 1958)

cards.title: Cheque you received bounced
steps: Collect BANK RETURN MEMO from your bank — keep original safe.
Send STATUTORY NOTICE to drawer within 30 DAYS via registered post.
Notice must demand payment within 15 DAYS.
If unpaid after 15 days: file complaint within 30 DAYS at JM First Class court of payee's location.
⏰ Missing ANY deadline = complaint dismissed. Strict timelines.
Court can award: cheque amount + interest + up to 2 years imprisonment OR fine up to 2× cheque amount.
Use template in our Docs tab → Cheque Bounce Notice (Sec 138 NI Act).
